finding: Large next.ts extraction can reach the required line budget faster than it can fully type all newly bounded projection modules, tempting agents to use transitional ts-nocheck surfaces during a governed split.
expectedBehavior: ATM refactor cards should distinguish temporary extraction seams from fully typed module boundaries, and should automatically open or recommend a follow-up hardening card when transitional ts-nocheck is introduced.
evidenceOrRepro: TASK-RFT-0031 split next.ts into route-resolution, prompt-results, and playbook-projection modules. The source and frozen runner validators pass, but prompt-results/playbook-projection were intentionally marked as transitional extraction surfaces to preserve behavior while meeting the <2,000-line source budget.
followUp: Add a post-split hardening card to remove ts-nocheck from extracted projection modules, restore explicit imported types, and add a validator that blocks transitional surfaces from surviving beyond the current refactor wave.
